name: A Bevy of Bagel
recipeIngredient: 4 1/2 cups All-Purpose Flour, or as needed
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, combine 1/2 cup of the warm water, sugar and yeast. Stir and let stand until foamy, about 10 minutes.
2. In a large bowl with an electric mixer, combine gluten flour, 4 1/2 cups all-purpose flour, salt, and remaining 1 1/2 cups warm water. Add yeast mixture and beat for 5 minutes on medium-low speed. Cover dough and let it rise until it collapses, about 2 hours.
3. Stir in the additional 2 cups of all-purpose flour gradually into the dough. Spread any remaining flour on a working surface and turn the dough out onto it. Knead until smooth and shiny.
4. Place dough in an ungreased bowl and let rise, covered, until doubled in bulk, about 1 hour or more. Punch down the dough. Turn out onto a board and cut into 12 pieces. Cover with a dry towel, then damp one, and let rest for 10 minutes.
5. Removing one piece at a time from under the towel, begin forming the bagel shapes. Roll each piece into a "snake" about 8 inches long. Moisten and overlap edges; pinch firmly together. The bagel should be a uniform ring with a center hole no smaller than 1 1/2".
6. As each bagel is finished, place it on a dry towel. When all are formed, place another dry towel, then a damp one, over them and let rise for about 30 minutes, or until "half-proofed," but not doubled.
7. While the bagels are rising, preheat the oven to 425°F. Make the water bath by combining water, sugar, and salt in a large pot and bring to a simmer.
8. When the bagels are ready, drop about 3 at a time into the simmering water. Keeping the water at a bare simmer, cook each side for 1 minute. Remove with a slotted spoon.
9. Place on a baking sheet lined (not greased) with baker's parchment. All should fit on the pan—expect a close fit. Bake in the center of the oven for 10 minutes; the bagels should be set but not browned. Turn over and return to the oven for 5 more minutes, or until bagels are browned. Cool on a rack.
10. Store in a plastic bag in the refrigerator up to 2 days. If longer, freeze.
